fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Virginia Occupational Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as an occupational therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Virginia - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: occupational therapist license reciprocity virginia, how to transfer occupational therapist license to virginia, occupational therapist compact virginia, virginia occupational therapist endorsement application

board.name: Virginia Board of Medicine — OT
board.mailing_address: Virginia Board of Medicine, Perimeter Center, 9960 Mayland Drive, Suite 300, Henrico, VA 23233-1463

quick_facts.exam_name: National Board for Certification in Occupational Therapy (NBCOT)

license_types.title: Occupational Therapist Registered (OTR)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice occupational therapy independently in Virginia.

license_types.title: Certified Occupational Therapy Assistant (COTA)
license_types.description: Requires an associate degree and practicing under the supervision of a licensed OTR.

steps.title: Create or Log In to the Online Licensing Portal
steps.description: Visit the Virginia Department of Health Professions (DHP) Online Licensing Portal (license.dhp.virginia.gov/apply/) and register a new account or log in if you are a returning user.

steps.title: Complete the Online Application
steps.description: Select the appropriate application type (e.g., 'Initial Licensure by Examination' or 'Licensure by Endorsement') for Occupational Therapy within the Online Licensing Portal, accurately complete all required sections, and pay the application fee.

steps.title: Submit Official Transcripts
steps.description: Arrange for your accredited occupational therapy program to send official transcripts directly to the Virginia Board of Medicine, verifying your degree and graduation date.

steps.title: Verify NBCOT Certification
steps.description: Ensure the National Board for Certification in Occupational Therapy (NBCOT) sends verification of your certification directly to the Virginia Board of Medicine.

steps.title: Submit Verification of Other Licenses (for Endorsement)
steps.description: If you are applying by endorsement, arrange for official verification of licensure to be sent directly from every state or jurisdiction where you have ever held an occupational therapy license to the Virginia Board of Medicine.

steps.title: Monitor Application Status
steps.description: Regularly check the status of your application through the Online Licensing Portal and promptly respond to any requests for additional information from the Virginia Board of Medicine.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NBCOT exam.

provisional_text: Virginia offers a temporary occupational therapy permit for new graduates who have completed an ACOTE-accredited program and are awaiting their NBCOT exam results. The temporary permit allows practice under the direct supervision of a licensed OTR. Contact the Virginia Board of Medicine — OT for current temporary permit requirements and duration limits.
reciprocity_text: Virginia is a member of the OT Licensure Compact (OTC). Occupational therapists holding an active compact privilege may practice in Virginia without a separate state license. For those not eligible for compact privileges, Virginia also accepts standard endorsement applications with proof of current NBCOT certification and license verification from your home state.

faqs.question: How much does a Virginia OT license cost?
faqs.answer: The total initial application and licensing fee is approximately $130. Check the board website for the latest fee schedule.

faqs.question: Is NBCOT certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NBCOT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across all states.

faqs.question: Does Virginia have reciprocity for occupational therapists?
faqs.answer: Virginia has enacted the OT Compact, but compact privileges are not yet issuing. For now, occupational therapists still apply by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my occupational therapist license to Virginia?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Virginia while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Virginia is a member of the OT Licensure Compact (OTC). Occupational therapists holding an active compact privilege may practice in Virginia without a separate state license. For those not eligible for compact privileges, Virginia also accepts standard endorsement applications with proof of current NBCOT certification and license verification from your home state.

official_links.registration_steps: Visit the Virginia Department of Health Professions (DHP) Online Licensing Portal. Click 'Register a Person' to create a new account or 'Login' if you already have one. Follow the prompts to set up your account and begin a new application.
